0.title: Rococo (Java)
0.short_description: A microservice-based virtual gallery where users can add artists, museums and paintings. Stack: Spring Boot, REST API, gRPC, Kafka, Selenide, Retrofit, PostgreSQL. Implemented DB, gRPC, API, UI, Kafka autotests and including JUnit Extensions. CI/CD via GitHub Actions.
0.description:
**Rococo** is a project that represents a virtual gallery built on a microservice architecture. Users can add artists, museums, and paintings. The project is rich with modern Java stack technologies and tools for test automation and CI/CD.

## 🛠️ Architecture & Technologies
*   **Backend**: Java 21, Gradle, Spring Boot (Hibernate, REST, gRPC, Apache Kafka)
*   **Infrastructure**: Docker
*   **Databases**: PostgreSQL
*   **Testing**: Java 21, Gradle, JUnit 5, Spring-JDBC, gRPC, Kafka, Retrofit 2, Selenide, Selenoid, Allure

## 🧩 Services
 * **Auth** - user registration and authorization
 * **Gateway** - routing
 * **Artists** - artists management
 * **Countries** - countries management
 * **Files** - image management
 * **Museums** - museums management
 * **Paintings** - paintings management
 * **Users** - user management
 * **Logs** - logs (for storing and later attaching logs to Allure Reports)
 * **Frontend** - client application

## 💻 What's Implemented
1.  **Backend** - All services are fully coded, including `unit` and `integration` tests (over 250 tests)
2.  **Testing** - Over 250 tests have been written for the `database`, `gRPC` service integration, `REST` APIs, `Kafka`, as well as `UI` and `Screenshot` tests, utilizing `JUnit 5 (Extensions, Resolvers)`
3.  **Environment** - Dockerization of the frontend, backend, and tests
4.  **CI/CD** - A GitHub Action workflow is written to run tests on pull requests, with informative test result messages posted directly in the pull request

## 🚀 Running the Project
Convenient test execution is implemented for different environments: `local`, `docker`, `ci`

### Prerequisites:
1) Docker, Java 21, and npm must be installed locally.
2) Generate a PAT token in GitHub and set the required environment variables `GITHUB_TOKEN_NAME` and `GITHUB_TOKEN` with read permissions for issues.

### Detailed Launch Instructions:
1) Locally: [Link to detailed information](https://github.com/arrnel/rococo?tab=readme-ov-file#%D0%B7%D0%B0%D0%BF%D1%83%D1%81%D0%BA-rococo-%D0%BB%D0%BE%D0%BA%D0%B0%D0%BB%D1%8C%D0%BD%D0%BE-%D0%B2-ide)
2) In Docker: [Link to detailed information](https://github.com/arrnel/rococo?tab=readme-ov-file#%D0%B7%D0%B0%D0%BF%D1%83%D1%81%D0%BA-%D1%82%D0%B5%D1%81%D1%82%D0%BE%D0%B2-%D0%B2-docker)
3) In CI:
    a) Fork the repository (if you haven't already)
    b) In the repository settings, add the necessary secrets and environment variables (Settings -> Secrets and variables -> Actions)
    c) In the navigation, go to the Actions section and select the desired action, e.g., Nightly run
    d) Run the workflow

## 👉 More Details
More information is available in the [original README](https://github.com/arrnel/rococo)

1.title: Bank (Java)
1.short_description: A microservices-based system for financial operations and currency exchange rate retrieval. Stack: Java 21, Kafka, OpenFeign, Swagger, Jackson. Includes Gateway (REST/Kafka), Currency (external API integration), Payment (entity creation), and module with automation Kafka tests.
1.description:
**Bank** is a project that represents a multicurrency payment service built on a microservice architecture. Users can create bank accounts, currency wallets, perform deposits, transfers, debits, and partial/full refunds. The project is rich in modern Java stack technologies and tools for test automation and CI/CD.

## 🛠️ Architecture and Technologies
*   **Backend**: Java 21, Gradle, Spring Boot (Hibernate, OpenFeign, Apache Kafka, Validation, OpenAPI)
*   **Infrastructure**: Docker
*   **Databases**: PostgreSQL
*   **Testing**: Java 21, Gradle, JUnit 5, Kafka

## 🧩 Services
 * **Currency** - service for fetching and storing currencies
 * **Payment** - bank accounts, currency wallets, transactions (refunds)
 * **Gateway** - routing

## 💻 What has been implemented in the project
1. **Backend** - developed microservices with integration via both REST and Kafka
2. **Testing** - developed automated tests for verifying Kafka integration of the payment service
3. **Environment** - building backend and tests in Docker
4. **CI/CD** - created a workflow for running tests in GitHub Actions on pull requests with an informative message about the test results within the pull request itself

## 🚀 Project Launch
Convenient test execution in different environments: `local`, `docker` has been implemented.
### Prerequisites:
1) Docker and Java 21 must be installed locally
2) Set the required environment variables

### Detailed launch instructions:
1) Locally: [Link to detailed information](https://github.com/arrnel/bank?tab=readme-ov-file#%D0%B7%D0%B0%D0%BF%D1%83%D1%81%D0%BA-rococo-%D0%BB%D0%BE%D0%BA%D0%B0%D0%BB%D1%8C%D0%BD%D0%BE-%D0%B2-ide)
2) In Docker: [Link to detailed information](https://github.com/arrnel/bank?tab=readme-ov-file#%D0%B7%D0%B0%D0%BF%D1%83%D1%81%D0%BA-%D1%82%D0%B5%D1%81%D1%82%D0%BE%D0%B2-%D0%B2-docker)
3) In CI:  
    a) Fork the repository (if you haven't already)  
    b) In the repository settings, add the necessary secrets and environment variables (Settings -> Secrets and variables -> Actions)  
    c) In the navigation, go to the Actions section and select the desired action, e.g., Nightly run  
    d) Run the workflow

## 👉 More Details
More information is available in the [original README](https://github.com/arrnel/bank)

2.title: Online Store (Java)
2.short_description: Automated UI/API Tests for automationexercise.com. Stack: Java 21, Playwright, RestAssured, Allure, Docker. Includes Dockerized execution, Allure reports, and JUnit 5 extensions.
2.description:
**AutomationExercise (Java)** is a test automation framework for the online store [automationexercise.com](https://automationexercise.com), built with Java. The project covers UI and API functionality: catalog, cart, checkout, registration, etc. It features flexible configuration for different environments and integration with a bug tracking system (GitHub Issues).

## 🛠️ Technologies & Tools
*   **Language & Build**: Java 21, Gradle
*   **Test Runner**: JUnit 5
*   **Testing**: RestAssured, Playwright
*   **Reporting**: Allure
*   **Infrastructure**: Docker, Playwright (built-in browsers)
*   **Integration**: GitHub API (for working with Issues)

## 💻 What's Implemented
1. **UI and API Tests** — covering main user scenarios
2. **Screenshot Tests** — visual page comparison to detect regressions (with support for Chromium, Firefox, WebKit browsers)
3. **Flexible Configuration** — switching between `local`, `docker`, and `ci` environments via environment variables
4. **GitHub Issues Integration** — automatic test disabling (`@DisabledByIssue`) if the corresponding bug is still open
5. **Allure Reports** — detailed test execution information with attached screenshots and videos

## 🚀 Running the Project
### Prerequisites:
1) Install Java 21 and Docker (for containerized execution)
2) Create copies of the screenshot folder: `src/.screen-output/screenshots/remote` → `local` and `docker` (for correct `screenshot` test operation)
3) On the first run, set the `TEST_REWRITE_ALL_SCREENSHOTS=true` variable to create reference screenshots
4) Generate a PAT token in GitHub (with read permissions for issues) and set the environment variables `GITHUB_TOKEN`, `GITHUB_TOKEN_NAME`, `GITHUB_ACCOUNT_NAME`, `GITHUB_REPO_NAME`

### Execution Methods:
1) Locally: [Link to detailed information](https://github.com/arrnel/automation-exercise/blob/master/README.md#%D0%B7%D0%B0%D0%BF%D1%83%D1%81%D0%BA-%D1%82%D0%B5%D1%81%D1%82%D0%BE%D0%B2-%D0%B2-ide)
2) In Docker: [Link to detailed information](https://github.com/arrnel/automation-exercise/blob/master/README.md#%D0%B7%D0%B0%D0%BF%D1%83%D1%81%D0%BA-%D1%82%D0%B5%D1%81%D1%82%D0%BE%D0%B2-%D0%B2-docker)
3) In CI (GitHub Actions):
    a) Fork the repository (if you haven't already)
    b) In the repository settings, add the necessary secrets and environment variables (`Settings` -> `Secrets and variables` -> `Actions`)
    c) In the navigation, go to the `Actions` section and select the desired action, e.g., `Nightly run`
    d) Run the workflow

## 👉 More Details
More information is available in the [original README](https://github.com/arrnel/automation-exercise)

3.title: Online Store (Python)
3.short_description: Automated UI/API Tests for automationexercise.com. Stack: Python 3.13, Pytest, Selene, Httpx, Allure, Docker. Includes Dockerized execution, Allure reports, pytest hooks and fixtures.
3.description:
**AutomationExercise (Python)** is a test automation framework for the online store [automationexercise.com](https://automationexercise.com), built with Python. The project covers UI and API functionality: catalog, cart, checkout, registration, etc. It features flexible configuration for different environments.

## 🛠️ Technologies & Tools
*   **Language & Dependencies**: Python 3.13, Poetry
*   **Test Runner**: Pytest
*   **Configuration**: Pydantic v2
*   **Testing**: Httpx, Selene
*   **Reporting**: Allure
*   **Infrastructure**: Docker, Selenoid (remote browser execution)

## 💻 What's Implemented
1. **UI and API Tests** — covering main user scenarios
2. **Screenshot Tests** — visual page comparison to detect regressions
3. **Local and Remote Execution** — integration with Selenoid, video recording, and VNC access
4. **Flexible Configuration** — switching between `local`, `docker`, and `ci` environments via environment variables or `.env` files
5. **Allure Reports** — comprehensive test execution information with attached screenshots and videos

## 🚀 Running the Project
### Prerequisites:
1) Install Python 3.13, Poetry, and allure-commandline (version 2.35.1 recommended)
2) Register a user on [automationexercise.com](https://automationexercise.com) and provide their details in the `.env` file
3) Generate a PAT token in GitHub (with read permissions for issues) and set the required environment variables `GITHUB_TOKEN_NAME` and `GITHUB_TOKEN`

### Execution Methods:
1) Locally: [Link to detailed information](https://github.com/arrnel/automation-exercise-py?tab=readme-ov-file#%D0%B7%D0%B0%D0%BF%D1%83%D1%81%D0%BA-%D1%82%D0%B5%D1%81%D1%82%D0%BE%D0%B2-%D0%B2-ide)
2) In Docker: [Link to detailed information](https://github.com/arrnel/automation-exercise-py?tab=readme-ov-file#%D0%B7%D0%B0%D0%BF%D1%83%D1%81%D0%BA-%D1%82%D0%B5%D1%81%D1%82%D0%BE%D0%B2-%D0%B2-docker)
3) In CI (GitHub Actions):
    a) Fork the repository (if you haven't already)
    b) In the repository settings, add the necessary secrets and environment variables (`Settings` -> `Secrets and variables` -> `Actions`)
    c) In the navigation, go to the `Actions` section and select the desired action, e.g., `Nightly run`
    d) Run the workflow

## 👉 More Details
More information is available in the [original README](https://github.com/arrnel/automation-exercise-py)
